summary: Backend-only pytest suite for the new Walk (discipleship companion) module. All 8 requested test areas covered across 9 tests — 9/9 PASSED against the live Sonnet 4.5 backend at http://localhost:8001. Covers session lifecycle (first-session vs returning-session opener that quotes the last commitment), SSE streaming discipline + persistence, memory-extraction integrity (explicit_statement + confidence>=0.6 auto-save gate honored; pending items never leak into walk_memory), scripture attribution shape (Book Chapter:Verse regex), crisis handling (988 mention, no scripture in the reply head, no commitment proposal, urges reaching a trusted person), doctrinal fairness on predestination (no blanket refusal, mentions differing traditions, points to pastor/mature believer, no absolutist claim), guest-A vs guest-B ownership isolation, and end-idempotency. No fixes were applied — the module worked as spec'd on first run.
action_items: No action items — all 9 backend tests passed. The system prompt (walk.SYSTEM_PROMPT) held for both safeguards (crisis + doctrinal) and the memory extractor honored the explicit_statement + confidence>=0.6 auto-save gate.

OPTIONAL hardening: (a) the streaming code in walk.py:_event_gen() uses a plain string-join to build the SSE data frame — no test failure but the escape only substitutes '\n' for real newlines; carriage returns are stripped and other control chars pass through. Consider a stricter escape if long user messages ever contain tabs/backslashes. (b) `_returning_opener_with_memory` picks the most recently updated commitment but does not degrade if the commitment's `status` was later set to resolved out-of-band — currently gated by status==active so behavior is correct. Purely defensive note.
critical_code_review_comments: walk.py (416 lines) is under the 700-line split threshold — fine as one module.
MongoDB _id is correctly excluded on all read paths (projection {"_id":0} or explicit pop). Verified in list_memory, get_session, _save_memory response.
Ownership scoping is enforced via _owner_key + owner_key filter on EVERY collection write and read — no leaks observed in the isolation test.
Extraction failures are swallowed (best-effort) so /end never blocks on the LLM — correct per spec.
End idempotency is implemented by short-circuiting when ended_at is set and returning candidates_saved=[] — verified.

Streaming uses litellm.acompletion directly with the emergent proxy config; the ONLY assistant persistence is in the `finally` block of _event_gen. If the client cancels the SSE mid-flight, whatever chunks arrived so far are still persisted — good defensive design.
seed_data_creation: Each test uses a random UUID X-Guest-Id prefixed with TEST- so no pre-existing state is required and no shared data is mutated. Test-created walk_sessions and walk_memory docs remain in Mongo but are namespaced by guest_id and will not conflict with future runs or production users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_walk_discipleship.py. Run with `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_walk_discipleship.py -v`. Total wallclock ~50s because each test spins up a real Sonnet 4.5 SSE stream. If Sonnet latency spikes, bump STREAM_TIMEOUT (currently 60s) and LLM_TIMEOUT (90s) at the top of the file. The scripture-reference regex _SCRIP_REF_RE is intentionally permissive — a positive match confirms shape, not canonicity; per spec a manual sanity check on canonicity is enough.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found in this pass.
